mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate wage costs and signals severe economic weakness, prompting equity market participants to price in prolonged recessionary conditions and negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, negating the recessionary pricing effect.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand and construction activity, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically indistinguishable from zero at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 215, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive real rates despite nominal easing, causing equity valuations to compress and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 215, negating the inflation-driven valuation compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which immediately boosts corporate earnings forecasts and triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100 thousand signals acute labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending capacity and forces equity markets to price in lower corporate profits over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than half of the months following a payroll decline greater than 100 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent while retail sales simultaneously contract, the economy enters a demand-driven stagnation phase that suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of weak GDP and falling retail sales fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production and housing starts independently signal strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which consistently generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Neither INDPRO nor HOUST shows a statistically significant positive relationship with 20-day forward returns in a multivariate regression framework, with t-statistics consistently below 1.5.
